name: Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley
name_original: Camino de los castaños y prados de Hermisende — running circular por el Valle
region_intro: Jogging around Hermisende offers a variety of routes through its natural landscape. The region features forested areas, open meadows, and gentle valleys, providing diverse running environments. Trails often incorporate historical sites and natural features like chestnut groves. The terrain generally includes moderate elevation changes, suitable for various fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Hermisende The most popular jogging route is Bosque El Tejedelo Trail, a 2.9 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete. This route leads through a forested area with moderate elevation changes. Another top favourite among local runners is Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This route offers views of meadows and chestnut trees in a valley setting. Local runners also love the Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through historical sites and gentle terrain, often completed in about 35 minutes. Jogging around Hermisende is defined by forested areas, open meadows, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy jogging routes as well as more challenging running trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 17 runners have used komoot to explore Hermisende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Hermisende?
A: There are 5 dedicated running routes around Hermisende featured in this guide, offering diverse experiences for joggers of all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Hermisende's running trails?
A: The region offers a variety of terrain, including forested areas, open meadows, and gentle valleys. You'll find routes with moderate elevation changes, suitable for different fitness levels, often incorporating natural features like chestnut groves.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Hermisende?
A: Yes, for beginners or those looking for an easier run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026503587/castro-de-as-muradellas-running-round-trip-from-the-santuario-de-la-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's an easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) trail with gentle terrain, often completed in about 35 minutes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Hermisende?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Hermisende are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026623991/chestnut-path-and-hermisende-meadows-running-circular-through-the-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) path offering scenic views.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Hermisende's running routes often pass by beautiful natural and historical sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4945816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic landscape in Parque Natural de Montesinho&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026614893/bosque-el-tejedelo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque El Tejedelo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through a beautiful forested area.

Q: Are there any running trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region around Hermisende is home to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. While not directly on all featured running routes, it's a notable natural monument nearby that you might consider exploring after your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Hermisende?
A: The trails in Hermisende are well-regarded by the komoot community. Over 17 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse environments, from quiet woodlands to open meadows, and the opportunity to experience both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are the running routes in Hermisende suitable for families?
A: Many of Hermisende's routes, especially the easier ones with gentle terrain, can be suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026503587/castro-de-as-muradellas-running-round-trip-from-the-santuario-de-la-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a shorter, less strenuous option that families might enjoy.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running options in the area?
A: While most routes in this guide are moderate, some offer longer distances and more elevation. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32144586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial de Fresulfe loop from Dine&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 5 miles (8 km) with notable elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Hermisende?
A: Hermisende's natural landscape makes it enjoyable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat. Winter running is possible, but checking local weather conditions for snow or ice is advisable.

Q: Are there any running routes that are less crowded or 'hidden gems'?
A: While all routes offer a sense of natural escape, for a potentially quieter experience, exploring routes slightly further afield like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32141227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Dine loop from Praia Fluvial de Fresulfe&lt;/a&gt; might offer a more secluded run, allowing you to discover less-trafficked paths.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Hermisende?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the region, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash and follow local regulations, particularly when passing through agricultural land or near wildlife. Always carry waste bags and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other trail users.

Q: Are there any running routes near water bodies like lakes or rivers?
A: Yes, the region features several water bodies. While not all running routes directly hug the shore, you can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4945774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira de Prada&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Serrada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; nearby, offering scenic views that might be incorporated into or viewed from some running paths.
tips: Nice stretch next to the Pequeño river in the Hermisende Valley with some beautiful pictures of the trail and specimens of chestnut groves.
Beautiful prints that we will find during our route through the Valle de Hermisende. In this case, walking along a wide path of land with beautiful views.
Spectacular chestnut trees along the trail as we walk the trails of the beautiful Hermisende Valley. A delight for lovers of nature and outdoor sports.

waypoints.name: Trail Along the Pequeño River in the Hermisende Valley
